title: Good Omens
creator: Neil Gaiman, Terry Pratchett
review:
Season 1 of Good Omens was an absolute joy to watch, Terry Pratchett's writing is just so inspired and full of tiny joys. While Neil Gaiman brings the grit and slight dread of living in a universe where all the decisions have already been made for you.

Season 2 was also a joy to watch, the story felt slightly disjointed but i still rather enjoyed it. I would never have expected Gabriel to Fall in love with Beelzebub.
Did the story feel good? Not particularly. But I adore watching Aziraphale, played by Michael Sheen, and Crowley, played by David Tennant, interact with each other.
The betrayal and rejection of Crowley at the end made me miserable for days and I couldn't wait for the third and final season to come out.

but..

Neil Gaiman just couldn't keep it in his pants.

After two years the allegations were dropped and charges were cleared but the damage was already done and the show had been canceled.
The actors and the rest of the team worked hard and were able to convince Amazon to allow them to produce a 90 minute movie in place of the six episodes the season was supposed to have.

Then on May 13, 2026, the movie released.

I anxiously watched it in-between university classes with my boyfriend over discord.
It was a great experience and the movie itself wasn't as bad as I originally expected it to be.
It was not a great movie by any stretch of the word, but I can appreciate it for what it is.
The story was rushed, the ending was controversial, a lot changed since season 1.
But I got closure for these characters that I hold so dear to my heart.

Aziraphale: the lazy gluttonous angel that just couldn't keep himself from having this higher than thou attitude towards everyone else.
Crowley: the good intentioned demon that didn't so much Fall as saunter vaguely downwards.
They both mean so much to me and i will forever keep them close to my religiously traumatized heart.

Through all the high highs and all the low lows, I love this show and highly recommend it to all who are willing to give it a try.

title: Daemons of the Shadow Realm
creator: Hiromu Arakawa
review: Hiromu Arakawa is an excellent writer. Her use of duality throughout the story has already made me reflect on the dualities within myself. Chaos and order, strength and vulnerability. I am looking forward to seeing where she takes this story.
